feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Implications of the Pessimistic Lower Limit on the Drake Equation

author.name: (habitable|habitability planet|planetary|world ...

entry.content:
Authors: M Baak, H Snoek - arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.02372, 2026

Abstract: ... makes its presence known on a habitable planet through emitting detectable signals. In essence ... life can develop on any habitable planet given the right (albeit unknown) conditions. ... refines our understanding of the possible existence of ...

entry.content:

entry.title: NP2 copular agreement in Czech presentational contexts

author.name: Edgar Onea

entry.content:
Authors: I Kučerová, E Onea - Papers from the Second International Workshop on the ...

Abstract: We discuss a class of classificatory NP-NP copular clauses, such as This tree is a linden tree. They display NP2 𝜑𝜑-feature agreement (copular and inter-sentential anaphoric agreement), even though NP2 is a predicate. We argue that this ...

entry.title: Emergence of Turbulence in a counterflow geometry of 2D Polariton Quantum Fluids

author.name: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence

entry.content:
Authors: L Depaepe, K Ouahrouche, A Amo, C Hainaut -arXivpreprintarXiv:2603.05125, 2026

Abstract: We numerically investigate the nonlinear dynamics of a two-dimensional exciton-polariton quantum fluid coherently driven by two counter-propagating laser beams. Using an exciton-photon coupled driven-dissipative Gross-Pitaevskii framework, we identify ...

entry.title: Young children rely on visual information to process degraded speech: Evidence from behavioural and neuroimaging measures

entry.title: Tight inapproximability of max-LINSAT and implications for decoded quantum interferometry

author.name: Jens Eisert

entry.content:
Authors: MJ Kramer, C Schubert, J Eisert - arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.04540, 2026

Abstract: We establish tight inapproximability bounds for max-LINSAT, the problem of maximizing the number of satisfied linear constraints over the finite field $\mathbb {F} _q $, where each constraint accepts $ r $ values. Specifically, we prove by a direct ...

entry.title: QGPU: Parallel logic in quantum LDPC codes

author.name: Jens Eisert

entry.content:
